answer:
Let the average annual earnings before December be \( A \).  
Total earnings for 11 months = \( 11A \).  
After December, new average = \( A - 1200 \), so total for 12 months = \( 12(A - 1200) \).  
Thus, \( 11A + 26800 = 12A - 14400 \), giving \( A = 41200 \).  
The new average = \( 41200 - 1200 = 40000 \).  

\boxed{C}
